stimulus: To casual observers, it does not appear that the leafy sea dragon relies on mimicry to deter ______blank the flamboyantly fringed fish drifts openly through shallow kelp beds rather than retreating into crevices. Researcher Akemi Tanaka of Flinders University has explained that the dragon’s elaborate appendages may in fact be precisely what conceals it.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. A semicolon correctly joins the first independent clause ("To casual observers...predators") and the second ("instead...crevices"). The comma after the adverb "instead" properly sets the transition off from the clause it modifies.

Choice B is incorrect because a comma alone produces a comma splice between two independent clauses. Choice C is incorrect because it fuses two independent clauses without sufficient punctuation. Choice D is incorrect because placing the semicolon after "instead" misassigns the transition, illogically attaching it to the previous clause.
